creator: david@wood2.org.uk
text:
[miwi@ and nivo+kw+ports.bfa274@is-root.com, maintainer of 
mail/dovecot-sieve added to the To line]


I was looking for this PR, hoping someone had beaten me to it.

This PR is to update the mail/dovecot-sieve port to 1.1.5 - which is 
under different maintainership to mail/dovecot.


The versions of mail/dovecot and mail/dovecot-sieve are inter-related - 
dovecot 1.1.x needs the dovecot-sieve 1.1 series. Now that mail/dovecot 
has been updated to 1.1.x, mail/dovecot-sieve is broken.

Hopefully the necessary maintainer approval can be sought and this 
committed as soon as possible. The current situation with incompatible 
dovecot and dovecot-sieve ports is not ideal considering how 
mission-critical dovecot for some users and that some dovecot 
installations (including those I'm responsible for) rely on sieve.


In the medium term, I wonder if dovecot-sieve should be incorporated 
into the mail/dovecot port. I was meaning to bring this up during the 
dovecot 1.1 RC period, but didn't have the time to do so.

Timo has his reasons to ship dovecot-sieve separately from the rest of 
dovecot - but with the versions of dovecot and dovecot-sieve 
interdependent, maybe dovecot-sieve should be an option in the dovecot 
port.


In the long term, there's new Sieve code under development which will 
hopefully be part of dovecot 1.2. As this is code developed specifically 
for dovecot, rather than the cyrus Sieve code ported to dovecot, it 
should then be incorporated into the main dovecot distribution.


Best wishes to you all,




David
-- 
David Wood
david@wood2.org.uk
